operating_state: Total revenue increased 9% to $63.7 billion driven by 7% comparable sales growth and 23 net new warehouses opened since prior year. E-commerce comparable sales accelerated 21% while warehouse ancillary businesses grew modestly. Operating cash flow increased to $6,008 from $5,382 in the prior year first half driven by net sales and membership fee growth.
cost_structure: Gross margin percentage improved 5 basis points to 10.85% driven by core merchandise performance, though excluding gasoline deflation the improvement was 4 basis points. SG&A expenses as a percentage of sales decreased 8 basis points to 9.06% primarily from improved warehouse productivity and operational leverage. Core merchandise gross margins faced pressure from increased supply chain costs to support higher inventory levels and category mix shifts.
material_events: The company declared quarterly dividend of $1.16 per share in February 2025, compared to prior year which included a special dividend of $15 per share. Share repurchases totaled $413 for 443,000 shares at an average price of $932.03 versus $322 for 528,000 shares in the prior year. Annual membership fees were increased in the U.S. and Canada effective September 1, 2024, contributing approximately 3% to membership fee revenue growth.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency translation adjustments created $414 million negative impact on comprehensive income for the first half, primarily from Canadian and Other International operations. Gasoline price deflation of 3% in Q2 and 8% year-to-date created net sales headwinds while benefiting gross margin percentage. Government tariff actions across multiple countries continue to affect merchandise costs with timing and degree dependent on specific goods and rates imposed.
forward_outlook: Management continues executing warehouse expansion with eight new warehouses opened in the first half and plans to open 20 additional new warehouses in the remainder of fiscal 2025. Membership fee increases are being recognized ratably over the one-year membership period with renewal rates of 93.0% in U.S./Canada and 90.5% worldwide.
uncertainty_profile: The company faces multiple legal proceedings including California labor law class actions, opioid litigation, privacy law violations related to website pixel trackers, and product liability cases. Federal investigations include a False Claims Act inquiry regarding prescription medications and EPA administrative complaints for pesticide regulation violations.
